description: Enforce authentication. server-core defines fastify.requireAuth() (jose jwtVerify -> sets request.auth) in packages/server-core/src/plugins/auth.ts, but it is attached to ZERO routes and there is no global auth hook, so request.auth is always undefined: JWTs are never verified and protected routes either 401 unconditionally or run with no identity. Add a global auth gate with an explicit public-route allowlist, and require auth on protected routes.
steps: In packages/server-core/src/plugins/auth.ts, add an opt-in global guard: a `requireAuthGlobal` option on createApp() that registers an `onRequest` hook calling the existing verify logic for all routes EXCEPT an allowlist (health, metrics, login/refresh/oauth callback, public webhooks).
Define the public-route allowlist as a configurable array (default: ['/health', '/healthz', '/ready', '/metrics', '/auth/login', '/auth/refresh', '/auth/oauth/*']). Support glob/prefix matching.
Update createApp() in packages/server-core/src/app.ts to accept { requireAuthGlobal?: boolean, publicRoutes?: string[] } and wire the hook after request-id/metrics but before route handlers.
In each app backend/app.ts (quantmail, quantchat, quantai, quantcalendar, quantdocs, quantdrive, quantmeet), enable requireAuthGlobal:true and pass that app's public routes.
Ensure request.auth is typed (FastifyRequest augmentation: auth?: { userId: string; ... }). Confirm the 49 `request.auth?.userId` read sites now receive a populated value when authenticated.

Add tests: (a) a protected route returns 401 with no/invalid Authorization header; (b) returns 200 and a populated request.auth.userId with a valid signed JWT (use a test key); (c) a public route (e.g. /health) is reachable without a token.
Document JWT_SECRET / JWT_AUDIENCE requirements (min 32 chars) in .env.example.
Run pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm build.
acceptance_criteria: requireAuth is actually enforced: at least one global hook or per-route preHandler verifies the JWT
Tests prove 401 (no token), 200 + populated request.auth (valid token), and public-route bypass
No route relies on request.auth being magically populated without the hook
